schools.summary: That Israel gave the Levites cities out of their own inheritance, at the commandment of the LORD: 'and the children of Israel gave unto the Levites out of their inheritance, at the commandment of the LORD, these cities and their suburbs' — the provision for God's ministers — 'and the children of Israel gave unto the Levites out of their inheritance, at the commandment of the LORD, these cities and their suburbs': the tribes gave the Levites cities and suburbs out of their own portions, as God commanded (the witnesses noting the significance — the provision God made for the Levites, who received no separate territory but cities scattered among all the tribes, sustained by the people they served, the Levites having the LORD as their inheritance and no land of their own, given cities among the tribes that they might dwell among the people and teach them, 'the priests the Levites... shall have no part nor inheritance with Israel... the LORD is their inheritance,' Deuteronomy 18:1-2, the lesson — that those who serve at the altar are to be provided for by the people, the minister sustained by those he serves, 'the LORD hath ordained that they which preach the gospel should live of the gospel,' 1 Corinthians 9:14, the truth that God's servants, having no portion in this world, are cared for by his appointment, the people giving of their inheritance to maintain the ministry, the figure — the minister of God whose portion is the LORD himself, not earthly possession, 'the LORD is the portion of mine inheritance,' Psalm 16:5, the truth that the Levites scattered through all Israel were a blessing and a witness in every tribe, the salt of the land, the figure of the ministry sustained by God's people and the LORD the minister's true inheritance, the call to provide for those who serve at God's altar, the assurance that God provides for his servants through his people, the figure of the provision for the ministers of God, who have no earthly portion but the LORD himself); Israel giving the Levites cities out of their own inheritance, the people providing for the ministers from their portions (the provision for God's ministers, Deuteronomy 18:1-2) — the gift (out of their inheritance); 'at the commandment of the LORD, these cities and their suburbs' (the cities given as the LORD commanded, the Levites having the LORD as their portion and sustained by the people, the minister living of the gospel, the LORD the minister's inheritance, 1 Corinthians 9:14; Psalm 16:5) — the figure (the minister whose portion is the LORD himself, the providence, the gift, the lot for the Kohathites to follow, vv.4) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does Israel giving the Levites cities out of their inheritance signify?

sides.position: The provision for the ministers of God, who have no earthly portion but the LORD himself - the Levites given no separate territory but cities scattered among the tribes, sustained by the people they served, having the LORD as their inheritance (Deuteronomy 18:1-2), those who serve at the altar provided for by the people (1 Corinthians 9:14), the minister's true portion the LORD himself and not earthly possession (Psalm 16:5)

schools.summary: The sum of all the Levitical cities, forty-eight, scattered through the possession of all Israel: 'all the cities of the Levites within the possession of the children of Israel were forty and eight cities with their suburbs' — the Levites scattered through Israel — 'all the cities of the Levites within the possession of the children of Israel were forty and eight cities with their suburbs': the whole number of the Levitical cities was forty-eight, spread among all the tribes (the witnesses noting the significance — the wisdom of God in scattering the Levites, his ministers, through all the tribes, that they might teach the law and be a witness and blessing in every part of the land, rather than dwelling apart in one territory, the Levites dispersed among the people, the forty-eight cities a means by which the knowledge of God was kept alive in every tribe, the prophecy of Jacob turned to blessing, 'I will divide them in Jacob, and scatter them in Israel,' Genesis 49:7, the lesson — that the curse of scattering pronounced on Levi was turned by God's grace into a blessing, the dispersion of the tribe made the means of spreading the knowledge of God, 'they shall teach Jacob thy judgments, and Israel thy law,' Deuteronomy 33:10, the truth that God's ministers are to be among the people, salt and light in every place, 'ye are the salt of the earth... ye are the light of the world,' Matthew 5:13-14, the figure — the church's ministers dispersed through the world, the witness of God in every place, the scattering that spreads the gospel, the truth that what man means or providence ordains as a scattering, God turns to the spreading of his truth, the figure of the ministers of God dispersed among the people, the salt of every tribe, the call to be a witness wherever God places us, the assurance that God uses the dispersion of his servants to spread his knowledge, the figure of the ministers of God dispersed among the people, the salt and light of every tribe); the Levitical cities spread through the possession of all the tribes, the Levites dispersed among the people (the Levites scattered, Genesis 49:7) — the cities (within all Israel's possession); 'were forty and eight cities with their suburbs' (the forty-eight cities the means by which the Levites taught the law in every tribe, the curse of scattering turned to blessing, the ministers salt and light in every place, the dispersion turned to blessing, Deuteronomy 33:10; Matthew 5:13-14) — the figure (the church's ministers dispersed through the world, the providence, the sum, the great summary of God's faithfulness to follow, vv.43) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does the scattering of the forty-eight Levitical cities through all Israel signify?

sides.position: The ministers of God dispersed among the people, the salt and light of every tribe - the wisdom of God in scattering the Levites through all the tribes that they might teach the law and witness in every place (Genesis 49:7), the curse of scattering pronounced on Levi turned by grace into a blessing and the means of spreading the knowledge of God (Deuteronomy 33:10), the ministers salt and light in every place (Matthew 5:13-14)

themes: All the cities of the Levites within the possession of the children of Israel were forty and eight cities with their suburbs
the sum of all the Levitical cities, forty-eight, scattered through all Israel (the crux of the ministers of God dispersed among the people, the salt and light of every tribe)
the wisdom of God in scattering the Levites through all the tribes, that they might teach the law and be a witness in every part of the land, the dispersion keeping the knowledge of God alive
the curse of scattering pronounced on Levi turned by grace into a blessing, the ministers salt and light in every place (Genesis 49:7; Deuteronomy 33:10; Matthew 5:13-14)

schools.summary: That each Levitical city had its suburbs round about: 'these cities were every one with their suburbs round about them: thus were all these cities' — each of the Levitical cities had its suburbs for the Levites' use (the these cities were every one with their suburbs round about them, each with suburbs, the thus were all these cities, so all of them, the figure, the cities each with its suburbs, the full provision for the Levites, the truth that every Levitical city had its surrounding land, the provision complete, Numbers 35:4, the figure of the full and equal provision for the ministers, each city furnished, the cities with suburbs, the provision set down, vv.41); each city with its suburbs (the provision, Numbers 35:4) — the suburbs (round about); 'thus were all these cities' (all the cities so furnished, the full provision, the figure, the full and equal provision for the ministers, the providence, the suburbs, the great summary of God's faithfulness to follow, vv.43) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: These cities were every one with their suburbs round about them: thus were all these cities
that each Levitical city had its suburbs round about
each Levitical city furnished with its suburbs for the Levites' use
the full provision for the ministers complete (Numbers 35:4)

schools.summary: That the LORD gave Israel all the land he sware to their fathers, and they possessed and dwelt in it: 'and the LORD gave unto Israel all the land which he sware to give unto their fathers; and they possessed it, and dwelt therein' — the promise fulfilled — 'and the LORD gave unto Israel all the land which he sware to give unto their fathers; and they possessed it, and dwelt therein': God gave Israel the whole land he had sworn to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, and they possessed and dwelt in it (the witnesses noting the significance — the faithfulness of God in keeping the oath sworn to the patriarchs more than four hundred years before, the promise made to Abraham now fully accomplished in the possession of the land, the land sworn to the fathers, 'unto thy seed will I give this land,' Genesis 12:7, now wholly given, the oath of God fulfilled to the letter, the lesson — that God keeps his covenant and oath through all generations, the promise to the fathers fulfilled to the children, 'he hath remembered his covenant for ever... the covenant which he made with Abraham, and his oath unto Isaac,' Psalm 105:8-9, the truth that no length of time wearies God's faithfulness, the promise of centuries kept in its season, the land possessed as sworn, the figure — the certainty of God's promises, the heavenly inheritance as sure as the earthly was given, 'he is faithful that promised,' Hebrews 10:23, the truth that what God swears he performs, the possession proving the promise, the figure of the faithful God who fulfils every oath to his people, the call to trust the promises of God, sure though long deferred, the assurance that God gives his people the inheritance he has sworn, the figure of the faithfulness of God in fulfilling the promise made to the patriarchs); God giving Israel the whole land he had sworn to the patriarchs, the oath to the fathers fulfilled (the promise kept, Genesis 12:7) — the gift (all the land sworn); 'and they possessed it, and dwelt therein' (Israel possessing and dwelling in the land, the promise of centuries fully accomplished, God keeping his covenant through all generations, a pledge of every promise sure, the covenant kept, Psalm 105:8-9; Hebrews 10:23) — the figure (the certainty of God's promises, the heavenly inheritance as sure, the providence, the fulfilment, the rest from all enemies to follow, vv.44) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does God giving Israel all the land he sware signify?

sides.position: The faithfulness of God in fulfilling the promise made to the patriarchs - keeping the oath sworn to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob more than four hundred years before (Genesis 12:7), God remembering his covenant through all generations (Psalm 105:8-9), no length of time wearying his faithfulness, the possession proving the promise and a pledge of every promise of God sure (Hebrews 10:23)

themes: And the LORD gave unto Israel all the land which he sware to give unto their fathers; and they possessed it, and dwelt therein
that the LORD gave Israel all the land he sware to their fathers (the crux of the faithfulness of God in fulfilling the promise made to the patriarchs)
the faithfulness of God in keeping the oath sworn to the fathers more than four hundred years before, the promise to Abraham now fully accomplished, God keeping his covenant through all generations
no length of time wearying his faithfulness, a figure of the certainty of all God's promises (Genesis 12:7; Psalm 105:8-9; Hebrews 10:23)

schools.summary: That the LORD gave Israel rest round about, and not a man of their enemies stood before them: 'and the LORD gave them rest round about, according to all that he sware unto their fathers: and there stood not a man of all their enemies before them; the LORD delivered all their enemies into their hand' — the rest from all enemies — 'and the LORD gave them rest round about, according to all that he sware unto their fathers: and there stood not a man of all their enemies before them; the LORD delivered all their enemies into their hand': God gave Israel rest on every side, subduing all their foes (the witnesses noting the significance — the complete rest and victory God gave his people, no enemy able to stand before them, the conquest and the rest alike his gift, fulfilling his oath to the fathers, the rest round about, the enemies all delivered into Israel's hand, the victory not by their own strength but by the LORD, the lesson — that God gives his people rest from their enemies, the victory wholly his work, 'the LORD delivered all their enemies into their hand,' not their own sword, 'they got not the land in possession by their own sword... but thy right hand, and thine arm,' Psalm 44:3, the truth that no foe can stand when God fights for his people, the rest and the conquest his gift, 'the LORD your God he it is that fighteth for you,' Joshua 23:10, the figure — the rest God gives his people from all their spiritual enemies, the victory of faith, and the eternal rest that remains, 'there remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God,' Hebrews 4:9, the truth that the rest of Canaan was a pledge of the greater rest, the enemies subdued a figure of the final triumph, the figure of the rest God gives his people from all their foes, the victory his gift, the call to rest in the God who subdues our enemies, the assurance that no enemy can stand before God's people, the figure of the rest God gives his people from all their foes, the victory wholly his gift); God giving Israel rest on every side as he had sworn, the rest his gift and the fulfilment of his oath (the rest from enemies, Psalm 44:3) — the rest (round about); 'and there stood not a man of all their enemies before them; the LORD delivered all their enemies into their hand' (no enemy able to stand before Israel, all their foes delivered into their hand by the LORD, the victory his and not their own, a figure of the rest that remains and the final triumph, the victory God's gift, Joshua 23:10; Hebrews 4:9) — the figure (the rest from all spiritual enemies, the eternal rest, the providence, the rest, the great summary that no good thing failed to follow, vv.45) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does the rest God gave Israel from all their enemies signify?

sides.position: The rest God gives his people from all their foes, the victory wholly his gift - the complete rest and victory God gave, no enemy able to stand, the victory not by their own sword but by the LORD (Psalm 44:3), God fighting for his people (Joshua 23:10), a figure of the rest from all spiritual enemies and the eternal rest that remains for the people of God (Hebrews 4:9)

themes: And the LORD gave them rest round about, according to all that he sware unto their fathers: and there stood not a man of all their enemies before them; the LORD delivered all their enemies into their hand
that the LORD gave Israel rest round about, and not a man of their enemies stood before them (the crux of the rest God gives his people from all their foes)
the complete rest and victory God gave his people, no enemy able to stand before them, the conquest and rest alike his gift fulfilling his oath, the victory not by their own sword but by the LORD
God fighting for his people so that no enemy could stand, a figure of the rest from all spiritual enemies and the eternal rest that remains (Psalm 44:3; Joshua 23:10; Hebrews 4:9)

schools.summary: The great summary, that not one good thing of all the LORD had spoken to Israel failed, but all came to pass: 'there failed not ought of any good thing which the LORD had spoken unto the house of Israel; all came to pass' — the perfect faithfulness of God — 'there failed not ought of any good thing which the LORD had spoken unto the house of Israel; all came to pass': not one of all God's good promises to Israel failed, but every one was fulfilled (the witnesses noting the significance — the absolute and perfect faithfulness of God, who fulfilled to the letter every good word he had spoken, not one promise falling to the ground, the whole accomplished, every good thing the LORD had spoken come to pass, nothing wanting, the faithfulness of God complete and entire, the lesson — that God is perfectly faithful to his every word, no promise of his failing, the believer assured that all God has spoken shall come to pass, 'not one thing hath failed of all the good things which the LORD your God spake concerning you... not one thing hath failed thereof,' Joshua 23:14, the truth that the faithfulness of God is the ground of all assurance, his every promise sure and certain, 'the word of the LORD is right; and all his works are done in truth,' Psalm 33:4, the figure — the certainty of every promise of God, fulfilled in Christ in whom all the promises are yea and amen, 'all the promises of God in him are yea, and in him Amen,' 2 Corinthians 1:20, the truth that what God has spoken he performs without fail, the past faithfulness the pledge of the future, the figure of the perfect faithfulness of God, every word fulfilled, the call to rest wholly on the sure word of God, the assurance that not one of God's promises shall ever fail, the figure of the perfect faithfulness of God, every word of his promise fulfilled); not one of God's good words to Israel failed, every promise standing and accomplished (the faithfulness complete, Joshua 23:14) — the nothing-failed (of any good thing); 'all came to pass' (every good thing came to pass, the whole accomplished, the faithfulness of God the ground of all assurance, fulfilled in Christ in whom all the promises are yea and amen, the sure word, Psalm 33:4; 2 Corinthians 1:20) — the figure (the certainty of every promise of God fulfilled in Christ, the providence, the faithfulness, the altar of witness by the eastern tribes to follow, ch.22) — Calvin, Keil, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does 'there failed not ought of any good thing... all came to pass' establish?

sides.position: The perfect faithfulness of God, every word of his promise fulfilled - not one of God's good promises to Israel falling to the ground, the whole accomplished to the letter (Joshua 23:14), the faithfulness of God the ground of all assurance, his every promise sure (Psalm 33:4), a figure of the certainty of every promise of God fulfilled in Christ in whom all are yea and amen (2 Corinthians 1:20)

themes: There failed not ought of any good thing which the LORD had spoken unto the house of Israel; all came to pass
the great summary, that not one good thing of all the LORD had spoken to Israel failed, but all came to pass (the crux of the perfect faithfulness of God)
the absolute and perfect faithfulness of God, who fulfilled to the letter every good word he had spoken, not one promise falling to the ground, the whole accomplished
the faithfulness of God the ground of all assurance, a figure of the certainty of every promise of God fulfilled in Christ in whom all are yea and amen (Joshua 23:14; Psalm 33:4; 2 Corinthians 1:20)
